Description: The MCP2561/2 is a second-generation, high-speed CAN transceiver that serves as the physical layer interface between a CAN protocol controller and a two-wire differential CAN bus. It supports data rates up to 1 Mb/s, implements ISO-11898-2/5 standards, and is designed for robust automotive applications. Key functionalities include differential transmission and reception, very low standby current, protection against bus faults and transients, and features for system-level management such as a VIO pin for I/O voltage level shifting (MCP2562) or a SPLIT pin for bus common-mode stabilization (MCP2561).

KeyParameters.**Supply Voltage (VDD)**: Not explicitly stated in provided text, but implied for 12V/24V systems; typical range for such transceivers is 4.5V to 5.5V.
KeyParameters.**Fault Protection**: Short-circuit to battery voltage, high-voltage transients, thermal shutdown (~+175°C)

Functions.FunctionDescription: Transmit Data Input from CAN controller. Low = Bus Dominant, High = Bus Recessive.
MCP2562: Logic I/O Supply Voltage (1.8V-5.5V). MCP2561: Ground.
Primary Supply Voltage for the transceiver.
Receive Data Output to CAN controller. Reflects bus state: Low = Bus Dominant, High = Bus Recessive.
MCP2561: Stabilizes common-mode voltage in split termination schemes. MCP2562: Logic I/O Supply Voltage (1.8V-5.5V).
Standby Mode Control. Low = Normal Mode, High = Standby Mode (Low Power).
